executiveSummary: The Rare Books Bot has 5 UI surfaces across 3 tech stacks with a critical investment inversion: the production-grade React SPA serves an internal metadata tool while the actual product (conversational discovery chatbot) is a 449-line Streamlit prototype. The recommendation is to consolidate into a single React application with 9 screens in 4 tiers, placing the chatbot at the center, over a 7-week migration.
keyDecisions: Build a single React application with the conversational discovery bot as the landing page and centerpiece
Use React (not Angular) — continuing the existing 3,160 lines of production-grade TypeScript
Retire both Streamlit applications after rebuilding functionality in React
Merge QA regression runner into CLI as subcommand
Consolidate dual Primo URL schemes into single configurable scheme
featuresToKeep: Coverage Dashboard with stat cards (from React Workbench)
Agent Chat with proposal tables (from React Workbench)
Candidate card layout with smart display (from Chat UI)
TP/FP/FN labeling with issue tagging (from QA Tool)
Gold set export and FN search (from QA Tool)
Two-phase conversation with confidence scoring (from API)
CLI parse_marc and query commands (from CLI)
techStack: React 19 + TypeScript + Vite 8 + Tailwind CSS 4 + TanStack Query/Table + Zustand + Radix UI + React Router 7
